title: potentilla recta subsp. auriflora
intro: Potentilla recta subsp. auriflora, also known as Golden Cinquefoil, is a perennial flowering plant in the rose family, characterized by its yellow flowers and hairy leaves. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The medicinal properties of Golden Cinquefoil are said to provide various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and supporting the immune system. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to treat a range of health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions.
